action.reasoning: Observation: Quickdraw accuracy improved from 0.1431 to 0.1589 with the stronger augmentation recipe, a meaningful gain, but the 8-epoch run took about 61 minutes and source-val remains only weakly predictive. Analysis: we now have a solid baseline near the expected ceiling for plain ERM, so the best remaining lever is inference-time robustness rather than another long training gamble. Quickdraw is line-drawing-like, and the model was explicitly trained with sketchified views, so test-time ensembling between the original image and a sketchified transform is a cheap, targeted way to improve domain alignment. We should first validate this locally on val by adding TTA options to validate.py and predict.py, then if promising submit eval on the same best checkpoint. Decision: add sketch-TTA and resize/flip TTA support to prediction/validation, run local validation sweeps on the current best checkpoint, and use the result to choose the next eval without retraining yet.
action.command: python validate.py --checkpoint checkpoints/baseline_aug/best.pt --tta_mode none && python validate.py --checkpoint checkpoints/baseline_aug/best.pt --tta_mode flip && python validate.py --checkpoint checkpoints/baseline_aug/best.pt --tta_mode sketch && python validate.py --checkpoint checkpoints/baseline_aug/best.pt --tta_mode all
